title: Fix SSR article bodies rendering as Loading fallback
description:
Why this issue exists: Live post pages render only the Loading fallback in static HTML because blog article HTML is assigned from data.renderedHtml only inside onMount. Crawlers and no-JS clients see an empty article body even though hydration can fill it later.

What needs to be done: Initialize article renderedHtml from prerendered load data during SSR, keep client-only feature initialization separate, and add validation that built sitemap HTML does not expose Loading fallback in article bodies for posts. Audit adjacent content detail routes that use the same pattern and fix high-impact pages.

Acceptance criteria: Built HTML for /blog/post/raksara-has-finally-migrated-to-sveltekit/ and /blog/post/extroverted-introvert-where-comfort-meets-change/ contains article content, not only Loading. SEO dist validation fails if sitemap blog post pages contain the Loading fallback.
close_reason: Moved blog post, portfolio detail, and generic page markdown bodies into build-time load rendering; client hydration now only initializes enhancements. Added dist validation that fails on Loading fallback or missing article body.

title: Remove duplicate and conflicting route head metadata
description:
Why this issue exists: The root layout emits generic title, description, canonical, OG image, and Twitter card tags for every route while individual pages emit their own page-specific tags. Search crawlers can receive duplicated canonical, description, og:image, and twitter tags, making snippets and canonical selection less deterministic.

What needs to be done: Define one route metadata source per page. Keep site-wide fallback metadata only where a child page does not provide route-specific metadata, add robots meta from the route indexability decision, and ensure blog/portfolio/detail pages produce a single canonical URL and coherent OG/Twitter tags.

Acceptance criteria: Built HTML for homepage, blog index, a blog post, portfolio index, a portfolio detail page, profile, and about has exactly one canonical link, one meta description, one robots meta, coherent og:url matching canonical, and no stale default OG image before the page-specific image.
close_reason: Removed root-level duplicate description/social metadata, scoped canonical fallback, added robots meta, and enforced unique canonical/description/robots tags in dist validation.

title: Wire SEO validation into the production build
description:
Why this issue exists: Raksara already has SEO validators, but the production build script only generates metadata and runs the SvelteKit build. Regressions in prerender routes, sitemap coverage, robots.txt, canonical tags, JSON-LD, and static HTML indexability can ship unnoticed, which directly affects Google indexing.

What needs to be done: Update the build workflow so pre-build SEO artifact validation runs after metadata generation and post-build static HTML validation runs after SvelteKit build. Strengthen the dist validator so it checks every prerendered/indexable route, not just a small blog sample, and fails on missing or noindex metadata for sitemap URLs.

Acceptance criteria: npm run build fails if any sitemap URL is missing built HTML, any built indexable HTML is missing title/description/canonical, any sitemap URL has robots noindex, JSON-LD is invalid, or robots.txt blocks a sitemap URL. The validation output summarizes checked route counts.
close_reason: Wired SEO validation into the build, expanded dist validation across every sitemap URL, and fixed /thoughts so sitemap/prerender/static output agree.

title: Fix hardcoded domain in robots.txt and validate sitemap URL matches actual host
description: robots.txt contains Sitemap: https://readynaz.com/sitemap.xml hardcoded. Since the host is dynamically configurable via config.site_url or PUBLIC_SITE_URL env var, the Sitemap directive should always reflect the actual configured domain. The build script (scripts/build-metadata.js) already reads the site URL from config — ensure robots.txt Sitemap line uses that same resolved URL and that both robots.txt and sitemap.xml consistently reference the same domain. Add a validation step that errors if the Sitemap URL in robots.txt does not match the site URL from config.

title: Add WebSite + Organization JSON-LD schema to homepage
description: The homepage currently has no structured data. Adding WebSite and Organization schemas helps search engines understand the site identity, enables sitelinks search box, and improves rich results eligibility. The schema should use the dynamically resolved siteUrl (config.site_url || config.url || page.url.origin) already used elsewhere in the layout.

title: Audit archive indexability and internal linking for deep content
description:
Why this issue exists: Raksara includes deep nested blog content, tags, categories, directories, thoughts, gallery, and pages. Some archive routes are intentionally noindex and excluded from the sitemap, but they still influence crawl paths to deep posts. Low index coverage can happen when important detail URLs are only reachable through noindexed or paginated client-side listings.

What needs to be done: Produce a route inventory comparing content metadata, internal links, sitemap URLs, prerender routes, robots.txt blocks, and noindex decisions. Decide which archives should stay noindex, which should be indexable, and whether directory pages need crawlable SSR links even if noindexed.

Acceptance criteria: A documented route matrix exists in Beads notes or project docs. Any route policy changes are implemented in build metadata, robots.txt, and page head tags consistently. Deep blog posts are reachable from at least one prerendered HTML page via normal anchor links.

title: Generate RSS and Atom feeds for content discovery
description:
Why this issue exists: Sitemap coverage helps crawlers, but feeds give Google, readers, and external services another fresh-content discovery channel. Raksara has structured blog metadata but no generated feed artifacts in the static output.

What needs to be done: Generate /feed.xml and /atom.xml from published blog posts during metadata build, include canonical post URLs, stable updated dates, summaries, author data, and absolute cover URLs where useful. Add robots/sitemap references only where appropriate and validate feed files exist and parse.

Acceptance criteria: Static builds emit valid RSS and Atom feeds using the configured site URL. Feed entries include all sitemap blog posts in newest-first order. SEO validation fails if feeds are malformed and warns if no posts are available.
close_reason: Generated RSS and Atom feeds from blog metadata, added feed discovery links, documented the feeds, and validated all 65 posts are present in both feeds.

title: Add config-driven Google site verification metadata
description:
Why this issue exists: Google indexing diagnosis usually depends on Search Console ownership. The app currently has no obvious config field that renders google-site-verification, so deployments may need manual HTML edits or DNS-only verification.

What needs to be done: Add a site config field for Google verification tokens, render a google-site-verification meta tag in the document head when configured, document the config shape, and include it in SEO validation as a non-failing warning when absent.

Acceptance criteria: A configured token appears exactly once as <meta name="google-site-verification" ...> in built HTML. Missing verification emits a validator warning with clear remediation. Documentation explains how to set the value.
close_reason: Added config-driven Google Search Console verification meta support, documented google_site_verification, and added a non-failing SEO validation warning when absent.

title: Custom component ::carousels(path, limit?) — responsive infinite carousel from content path
description:
## Why
The site needs a visually rich carousel shortcode for featuring content (e.g. latest posts, selected portfolio) on landing pages. Authors embed `::carousels(path)` in markdown and get a fully responsive, animated carousel without writing any HTML.

## What needs to be done

### Parsing
- Extend the custom component parser to recognise `::carousels(path)` and `::carousels(path, N)`
- Same path + limit semantics as ::cards

### Data loading
- Resolve and sort pages by most recent date, same as ::cards
- If total > limit, truncate and flag showMore=true

### Visual — carousel layout
Three-card stage composition at all times:
- **Left partial card**: 35% width visible on the left edge, foremost-left side fades out with a CSS gradient mask (not the card itself fading, only the visible edge)
- **Center card**: full width, larger/more prominent (scale or z-index elevation), fully visible, active indicator
- **Right partial card**: 35% width visible on the right edge, foremost-right side fades out symmetrically
- No fixed card height variation — images use the same fixed-ratio container + object-fit cover as the gallery (LQIP blur-up, srcset from image manifest) so every card is the same height regardless of source image dimensions
- Cover image takes up the top ~60% of the card; title, description, and date below
- If no cover image: card fills with a neutral placeholder background, text still shown

### Interaction
- Prev / Next buttons (accessible, with aria-labels)
- Infinite loop: wraps seamlessly in both directions
- Auto-advance with a sensible interval (~5 s); pauses on hover or focus
- Clicking any card navigates to the page (center card direct, side cards advance first then navigate, or single click goes straight through — either is fine)
- Touch/swipe support on mobile

### Show More
- If showMore=true, render a 'Show more' button below the carousel
- Links to the section index for the given path

### Responsiveness
- Mobile (< 640 px): center card full width, side cards ~25% peeking; or collapse to single card with dots indicator — whichever looks better
- Tablet / desktop: standard three-panel layout

### Admin page integration
- Register ::carousels in the admin component palette the same way ::components and ::cards are registered
- Syntax hint, param descriptions, live preview stub

### Image handling — must match gallery approach
- Fixed-ratio image container (e.g. 16:9 or configurable), object-fit cover
- LQIP blur-up placeholder via the image manifest
- Responsive srcset built from the image manifest
- Result: all carousel cards identical height, no layout shift, looks good on any image aspect ratio

## Acceptance criteria
- `::carousels(blog/)` renders all blog posts as an infinite-loop carousel
- `::carousels(portfolio/, 4)` shows up to 4 portfolio items; Show More appears if there are more
- Auto-advance pauses on hover/focus and resumes on leave
- Swipe works on mobile
- Cards are keyboard-navigable (arrow keys advance the carousel)
- Show More button links to the correct section index
- Admin palette shows the component with correct syntax and params
- No card height variation regardless of whether a page has a cover image or what aspect ratio that image has

title: Custom component ::cards(path, limit?) — responsive card grid from content path
description:
## Why
The site needs a reusable shortcode to embed a card grid of content from any given path (e.g. blog/, portfolio/) inside markdown pages, similar to how ::components works today. This enables content authors to compose landing pages and cross-link sections without touching code.

## What needs to be done

### Parsing
- Extend the custom component parser to recognise `::cards(path)` and `::cards(path, N)` syntax
- `path` is a content path prefix (e.g. `blog/`, `portfolio/`)
- `limit` is an optional integer; when omitted show all

### Data loading
- At render time, resolve all pages whose slug starts with the given path
- Sort by most recent date (updated → modified → date)
- If total > limit, truncate and flag showMore=true

### Visual — card items
- Each card is clickable and links to the correct page route
- Card contents (in order): cover image (if present, using the same LQIP + responsive approach as gallery), page title, page description/summary, date
- No cover image → card still works, just no image slot
- Cards are arranged in a responsive grid: 1 col mobile, 2 col tablet, 3 col desktop
- Card hover: subtle lift/shadow

### Show More
- If showMore=true, render a 'Show more' button below the grid
- Clicking it navigates to the section index page for that path (e.g. /blog)

### Admin page integration
- Register ::cards in the admin component palette exactly as ::components is registered today
- Show syntax hint, required/optional params, and a live preview stub in the admin UI

### Image handling
- Reuse the gallery image approach: fixed-ratio container, object-fit cover, LQIP blur-up placeholder
- Srcset / responsive-image manifest wired up the same way as article covers and gallery cards
- No card height variation from image aspect ratio differences

## Acceptance criteria
- `::cards(blog/)` renders a grid of all blog posts sorted newest first
- `::cards(portfolio/, 6)` renders up to 6 portfolio items with a Show More button if there are more
- Cards are keyboard-navigable and accessible (role=article, alt text on images)
- Works correctly on 320px, 768px, and 1280px viewport widths
- Admin palette shows the component with correct syntax and params

title: Add Person JSON-LD schema for author entity
description: Blog posts reference an author name in BlogPosting JSON-LD but there is no standalone Person schema for the author. Adding a Person entity (with name, url, sameAs social profiles) enables Google Knowledge Panel association and improves author authority signals. The Person schema should be injected on the homepage or /profile page and cross-referenced in BlogPosting schemas via @id. Author data should come from config (config.author or dedicated author fields).

title: Add BreadcrumbList JSON-LD schema to blog post and portfolio pages
description: Blog post pages (/blog/post/[slug]) and portfolio item pages (/portfolio/[slug]) do not have BreadcrumbList structured data. Adding breadcrumb schema improves navigation clarity in search results (breadcrumb trail in SERP snippets) and helps Google understand site hierarchy. The schema should reflect the actual URL path: Home > Blog > [Post Title] for posts, and Home > Portfolio > [Item Title] for portfolio items.

title: Create Search Console indexing remediation runbook
description:
Why this issue exists: Code can make pages crawlable and indexable, but Google indexing still needs operational checks: property verification, sitemap submission, URL inspection, canonical selected by Google, coverage reasons, and crawl stats. Without a runbook, deployment issues can look like code bugs.

What needs to be done: Create a concise operational runbook for readynaz.com covering Search Console verification, sitemap submission, sample URL inspection, robots/canonical checks, expected wait times, and how to map Google coverage reasons back to Raksara routes.

Acceptance criteria: The runbook lists exact URLs to inspect, expected results for sitemap/robots/canonical/noindex, and a step-by-step checklist for requesting reindexing of priority pages after deployment.

title: Add image sitemap to improve image discoverability
description: The current sitemap.xml only lists page URLs with no image metadata. Adding image sitemap extensions (image:image elements inside existing url entries) allows Google Image Search to discover, index, and attribute images. Blog posts and portfolio items all have cover images that should be included. The image sitemap data should be generated by build-metadata.js alongside the existing sitemap, using cover image URLs extracted from blog-index.json and portfolio.json.

title: Add DNS prefetch and preconnect for third-party origins
description: The site loads resources from third-party origins (Google Fonts, Google AdSense, Cloudflare Turnstile, Giscus comments) but the layout does not include dns-prefetch or preconnect hints. Adding these in +layout.svelte reduces connection setup latency for the most critical third parties. Priority order: fonts (render-blocking), AdSense (revenue), Giscus (comments). Should be conditional on whether each feature is configured (e.g. only add AdSense preconnect if config.adsense is set).

title: Add per-section default OG images for blog, portfolio, and profile
description: Currently all pages fall back to a single default OG image (og/defaults/blog-landscape.jpg). Adding per-section default images improves social sharing appearance when pages lack a specific cover image. Suggested: separate defaults for blog index, portfolio index, and profile/about pages. These should be referenced in the layout or section-level layouts when no page-specific OG image is available. The image paths should come from config or a static convention (og/defaults/[section]-landscape.jpg).

title: Make robots.txt AI crawler blocklist config-driven
description: The list of AI crawlers blocked in robots.txt (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended, CCBot, Bytespider, Amazonbot, etc.) is hardcoded in build-metadata.js. New AI crawlers appear frequently and maintaining this list requires code changes. The blocklist should be driven by a config option (e.g. config.block_ai_crawlers: true/false or a list of user-agent names) so site operators can update it without touching build scripts. When block_ai_crawlers is true, apply a curated default list; when false, omit all AI-specific rules.
